name: R Seurat Visium spot/image overlay
description: Validate a Seurat Spatial assay, image, scale factors, and barcode-coordinate alignment, then return a coordinate-faithful SpatialDimPlot or SpatialFeaturePlot object.

parameters.name: object
parameters.description: Caller-supplied trusted Seurat object; it is validated and not modified.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Use SpatialDimPlot for identities/metadata or SpatialFeaturePlot for assay features.

parameters.name: assay
parameters.description: Existing spatial-registration assay associated with the selected image.

parameters.name: feature_assay
parameters.description: Existing normalized assay used only for feature values; NULL uses the spatial-registration assay. Its barcodes must exactly match the selected image coordinates.

parameters.name: image
parameters.description: One image name; required explicitly when the assay has multiple images.

parameters.name: group_by
parameters.description: Metadata grouping for identity mode; NULL uses active identities.

parameters.name: features
parameters.description: Existing assay features for feature mode; no expression values are recomputed.

parameters.name: cols
parameters.description: Optional identity palette passed to SpatialDimPlot.

parameters.name: crop
parameters.description: Crop to tissue coordinates using Seurat's spatial plotting contract.

parameters.name: label
parameters.description: Add Seurat identity labels in identity mode.

parameters.name: label_repel
parameters.description: Use Seurat label repulsion in identity mode.

parameters.name: image_scale
parameters.description: Existing image scale whose factor must be present and positive.

transformations.method: Seurat GetTissueCoordinates with declared image scale
Existing data layer from the explicitly selected feature assay; no normalization or assay copying
Seurat SpatialDimPlot or SpatialFeaturePlot without manual coordinate reconstruction

supports_claims: Shows the displayed identities or feature values at registered spots in the selected tissue image.
does_not_support: Does not establish spatial enrichment significance, registration accuracy beyond contract checks, cell-type deconvolution, biological replication, mechanism, or causality.
required_statistics: none for descriptive overlay; report spatial statistics and replicate structure separately for inference
preferred_companions: replicate-aware spatial summary when inference is intended
safer_alternatives: separate tissue image and quantitative table when overlay occlusion prevents reliable reading

visual_fingerprint.final_size_behavior: requires original and final-size checks of spot alignment, legend, tissue detail, and clipping
visual_fingerprint.review_note: An independent upstream Recipe harness rendered a trusted 2,695-spot Visium Seurat object; cluster and Hpca/Ttr original/final-size previews passed hash-bound native review. This does not validate the workflow that produced the object.

notes: Formal preflight passed against the pinned task-local Seurat 5.5.0 library after restoring native Windows X64 architecture in the child environment without mutating the parent.
The synthetic contract and barcode-mismatch guard passed in the pinned Seurat 5.5.0 task-local environment using an explicit Windows completion marker.

The independent upstream harness exited zero with no warning/error-pattern matches on a trusted real 10x Mouse Brain Sagittal-Anterior Seurat object; 2,695 Spatial-assay, image, and coordinate barcodes had zero six-way set differences.
Feature overlays explicitly separate the Spatial registration assay from the normalized feature assay and fail closed when a normalized data layer is absent.
All four real original/final-size previews received terminal KEEP decisions in native review; see validation-evidence.json.
The real execution validates this Recipe on the trusted object only; it does not validate the workflow or wrapper that produced that object.
